description: GitHub Actions OIDC subjects against AWS IAM role trust-policy conditions (StringEquals / StringLike). Seed tranche: exact vs wildcard semantics, the ? single-char wildcard, org- and repo-wide over-permission, pull_request subjects, and the classic-vs-immutable subject format break.

vectors.description: Subject pinned to one branch of one repo, matched with StringEquals. The tight baseline.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Exact match on a single protected branch of a single repo.

vectors.description: Same tight subject, but written with StringLike and no wildcard: StringLike degrades to exact.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: No wildcard present, so StringLike matches the exact subject only.

vectors.description: Trailing * after the repo admits any ref, tag, environment, or pull_request of that repo.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Repo-wide: every ref, tag, environment, and pull_request of this repo satisfies the condition.

vectors.description: Wildcard in the repo position lets ANY repository in the org assume the role. IAM * is not path-aware, so it spans the '/' and ':' delimiters.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/unrelated-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Org-wide trust: any repository under octo-org, including one created by anyone with repo-create rights, can assume the role. This is the pattern Checkov CKV_AZURE_249's repo regex passes.

vectors.description: A pull_request-triggered token matched by a repo-wide wildcard. Fork PR code can mint this subject.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:pull_request

judgment.reason: The condition admits the pull_request subject, so code from a pull request (potentially from a fork) can assume the role.

vectors.description: A different branch of the same repo is correctly rejected by an exact StringEquals condition.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/feature-x

judgment.reason: Only the pinned branch matches; other branches are denied.

vectors.description: Immutable subject (embedded owner/repo IDs) matched by a condition written in the same immutable form.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org@123456/octo-repo@7891011:ref:refs/heads/main

judgment.reason: Exact match on an immutable subject; the embedded IDs pin the identity across renames.

vectors.description: The July 15 breakage from the cloud side: a repo created after the immutable cutover mints an immutable subject, but the trust policy still carries the classic (no-@id) pattern. It stops matching. This is silent deployment breakage, not just a lint nit.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org@123456/octo-repo@7891011:ref:refs/heads/main

vectors.description: The ? wildcard matches exactly one character. Demonstrates StringLike's second, less-known metacharacter.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/octo-repo:ref:refs/heads/main

vectors.description: Under StringEquals, * is a literal character, not a wildcard. A StringEquals condition of 'repo:octo-org/*' matches only the literal string, never a real subject. The operator, not the string, decides.
vectors.subject: repo:octo-org/unrelated-repo:ref:refs/heads/main
